title: Onboarding wizard doctor — diagnostics-first onboarding detects and remediates a broken state

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Hub app running. A Unity project in a deliberately broken state: the bridge package is installed but the bridge assembly is NOT compiled (e.g. delete Library/ScriptAssemblies/*.dll or introduce a compile error in a project script). The Unity Editor may be open (showing compile errors) or closed. This scenario validates the wizard's diagnostics-first path: it runs the doctor, classifies the failure, and offers remediation.

steps.title: Put the project into a broken-bridge state

patches.content: public class BreakCompile { void Bad() { this is not valid C#; } }

steps.title: Open the onboarding wizard — diagnostics-first path
steps.body: In the Hub app, open the project. The wizard runs its doctor check before showing preset options. Observe the diagnostics panel: it should classify the failure as 'bridge compile failure' (not 'Unity not running' or 'package missing').

steps.title: Expected outcomes (diagnosis)
items: The doctor surfaces a 'compile failure' diagnosis (not a generic 'bridge offline')
The diagnosis names the offending script file (BreakCompile.cs) and the CS error code/line
The wizard offers a remediation action: 'Open the script in your editor' or 'Delete the broken script'
The preset picker is deferred until the bridge is healthy (diagnostics-first)

steps.title: Remediate — delete the broken script and confirm the bridge recovers
Expected outcomes (recovery)
items: After the recompile settles (~5-15s), ping returns isError:false
The wizard advances to the preset picker (bridge is now healthy)

steps.title: Ensure the broken script is gone
Paste the agent output (script_delete + ping + read_compile_errors envelopes)

reset.note: Optional scenario. The wizard diagnostics-first path is Hub UI; the remediation + recovery confirmation is agent-driven (script_delete + ping + read_compile_errors). Validates the doctor's failure classification (compile failure vs offline vs missing package) and the deferred-preset-picker UX.
